title: 10 best herbal capsules for nail fungus

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Germander capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain a potent combination of herbs that work synergistically to combat fungal infections. The capsule's active ingredients, including geranium and other plant extracts, inhibit the growth of fungi, thereby reducing the severity of fungal infections and promoting healthy nail growth. Additionally, herbal germander capsules have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate any discomfort or pain associated with fungal infections, allowing for a more comfortable and effective treatment experience.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Germander has been shown to exhibit antifungal activity against various fungal species, including Candida and Trichophyton, which are common causes of nail fungus.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Germander's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce inflammation and irritation caused by fungal infections, promoting a healthier environment for the nails.
Antioxidant properties: Germander's antioxidant compounds can help protect the nails from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to nail fungus development.
remedy_constituents: Rosmarinic Acid: This phenolic compound has been found to exhibit antifungal properties, which can help inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens responsible for nail fungus.
Nepetalactone: As a natural terpene, nepetalactone has been shown to have antifungal and antibacterial activities, contributing to the reduction of fungal infections in the nail.

Teuchamaedrys flavonoids: These flavonoids possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help mitigate the oxidative stress and inflammation associated with nail fungus, promoting a healthy nail environment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their antioxidant and antimicrobial properties, which help combat fungal infections.
Roots: They are used for their anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to soothe and heal the affected nails.
Barks: They are used due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to prevent the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause nail infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the dried leaves of teucrium chamaedrys by gently collecting the desired amount in a clean container.
Dry the harvested leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 1-2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powdered leaves with a natural binder such as gelatin in a 1:2 ratio.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture using a spoon and seal the capsules.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion capsules helps with nail fungus because of its high concentration of antioxidants, flavonoids, and fatty acids that work together to combat fungal infections. The capsule's natural antibacterial properties help to reduce inflammation and promote healthy circulation, allowing for the proper growth of new nail tissue. Additionally, dandelion's ability to stimulate liver function can aid in the removal of toxins that may contribute to fungal infections, making it a holistic approach to treating nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Dandelion contains antifungal compounds like taraxasterol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal species, including those that cause nail fungus.

Anti-inflammatory agents: Dandelion's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation and swelling in the nail bed, making it easier for the nail to grow out healthy and unaffected by fungus.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in dandelion capsules can help protect the nail cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of fungal infections and slow down the healing process.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have been shown to possess antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections that cause nail fungus.
Taraxasterol: This triterpenoid has been found to exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate symptoms associated with nail fungus, such as redness and swelling.
Luteolin: This flavonoid has been reported to have antifungal and antioxidant properties, which can help protect the nail from further damage and promote the growth of healthy nail tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Taraxacum officinale are used due to their high content of inulin, a prebiotic that has antifungal properties.
Leaves: The leaves of Taraxacum officinale are used because they contain flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to exhibit antifungal activity.
Seeds: The seeds of Taraxacum officinale are used due to their high content of inulin and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Dry the taraxacum officinale roots and leaves in a low-temperature dehydrator at 95 degrees fahrenheit for 24 hours.
Grind 10 grams of dried taraxacum officinale into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of vegetable cellulose in a ratio of 1:1 to create a uniform blend.
Fill 200-piece gelatin capsules with 0.5 gram of the herbal powder blend using a capsule filler.
Store the taraxacum officinale capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold capsules helps with nail fungus because of its potent antifungal properties. The herb, also known as Calendula officinalis, has been traditionally used to treat various fungal infections, including those that affect the nails. The capsules contain a concentrated extract of the herb's active compounds, which are absorbed into the bloodstream and carried directly to the affected areas, where they can help to combat fungal growth and promote healthy nail development.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: Pot Marigold's antifungal compounds, such as triterpenoids and flavonoids, inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause nail fungus, helping to reduce infection and promote healing.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Calendula's anti-inflammatory effects can help soothe and calm the inflammation associated with nail fungus, reducing redness, swelling, and discomfort.

Wound-healing promotion: Pot Marigold's wound-healing compounds, including saponins and flavonoids, promote collagen synthesis, improve tissue strength, and accelerate the healing process of damaged nails infected with fungus.
remedy_constituents: Sesquiterpenes: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antifungal properties, which can help combat fungal infections such as nail fungus.
Phenolic acids: They possess antimicrobial and antifungal activities, which can aid in preventing the growth and spread of fungal pathogens that cause nail fungus.
Carotenoids: These pigments have been found to exhibit antioxidant and antifungal properties, which can help protect the nail from oxidative stress and fungal infections.
remedy_parts: Flowers: The flowers are the primary part used to make capsules for nail fungus because they contain high concentrations of sesquiterpene lactones, which have antifungal properties.
Seeds: The seeds are also used due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help combat fungal infections.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make capsules for nail fungus because they contain triterpene saponins, which exhibit antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry the calendula officinalis flowers in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 500 milligrams of the dried powder with 100 milligrams of cellulose or other capsule filler.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the mixture using a small spoon or capsule filler.
Store the capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salad burnet capsules helps with nail fungus because of its unique combination of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. These nutrients have been shown to possess potent antifungal properties, which can help combat the growth of fungal infections that cause nail discoloration, brittleness, and other symptoms associated with onychomycosis. By targeting the underlying causes of nail fungus, salad burnet capsules can promote healthy nail growth and restore a clear, strong appearance to the nails.
remedy_properties: Antifungal activity: The capsules contain saponins, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, including those that cause nail fungus, by disrupting their cell membranes.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sanguisorba minor has anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce swelling and redness associated with nail fungus infections, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth.

Antioxidant activity: The capsules contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which can neutralize free radicals and protect the nails from oxidative damage caused by fungal infections, promoting healthy nail development.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: These compounds may help inhibit the growth of fungi and reduce inflammation, which can contribute to the development of nail fungus.
Phenolic acids: They have antimicrobial properties, which can help combat fungal infections by preventing the growth and spread of fungi on the nails.
Flavonoids: These compounds may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, creating a less favorable environment for fungal growth and potentially aiding in the recovery of nail health.
remedy_parts: Roots: Roots of Sanguisorba minor are used to make capsules for nail fungus due to their antimicrobial properties.
Leaves: Leaves of Sanguisorba minor are used to make capsules for nail fungus due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Stems: Stems of Sanguisorba minor are used to make capsules for nail fungus due to their potential to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50 grams of the fresh aerial parts of the plant at the optimal stage of flowering.
Dry the harvested plant material in a single layer at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 20 grams of the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mill.
Mix the powder with 5 grams of gelatin and 50 milliliters of water to create a uniform suspension.
Fill gelatin capsules with 500 milligrams of the suspension and seal immediately.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has antifungal properties. This compound works to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail infections, promoting healthy nail growth and improving the overall appearance of the nails. Additionally, licorice root extract has been shown to increase the production of antioxidants in the body, which helps to combat free radicals that can contribute to fungal growth. By using herbal licorice capsules regularly, individuals may be able to alleviate symptoms of nail fungus and promote healthy nail development.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice root has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and irritation caused by fungal infections, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth.

Antifungal properties: The glycyrrhizin present in licorice root exhibits antifungal activity against various types of fungi, including those that cause nail fungus, helping to inhibit their growth and spread.
Immune-modulating properties: Licorice root has been shown to stimulate the immune system, enhancing its natural defense mechanisms against infections, which can help combat nail fungal infections.
remedy_constituents: Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin may help with nail fungus by inhibiting the growth of fungal pathogens, such as Trichophyton mentagrophytes, and reducing inflammation in the affected area.

Liquiritigenin: This isoflavone may contribute to the antifungal properties of Glycyrrhiza glabra by disrupting the cell membrane of fungal cells, ultimately leading to their death and preventing the spread of the infection.

Licoricidin: This phenolic compound may help alleviate symptoms of nail fungus by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation, as well as exhibiting antifungal activity against various fungal species, including those that cause onychomycosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Glycyrrhiza glabra roots are commonly used in capsule form for nail fungus due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has antifungal properties.
Roots: The roots also contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that help to inhibit fungal growth and promote healthy nail growth.
Roots: Additionally, the roots have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe and calm irritated skin and nails affected by fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 250 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra root powder and set aside for later use.
Mix 250 grams of the powdered root with 250 grams of starch and 50 grams of gelatin powder.
Combine the mixture with 50 grams of water and 50 grams of glycerin to form a dough.
Use a capsule filling machine to fill the dough into empty gelatin capsules.
Seal the capsules and label them according to the recommended dosage of 250-500 milligrams twice a day.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tree turmeric capsules helps with nail fungus because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties. The curcuminoids present in turmeric have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi, reducing inflammation and promoting healthy nail growth. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of turmeric help to reduce oxidative stress and promote a healthy environment for nail regrowth. remedy_properties: Curcuminoids: Turmeric's active compound, curcuminoids, have potent antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help combat fungal infections such as nail fungus by inhibiting fungal growth and reducing inflammation.

Cineole: Cineole, a major constituent of turmeric essential oil, has been shown to possess antifungal and antimicrobial activities, which can help prevent the spread of fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.

Kaempferol: Kaempferol, a flavonoid present in turmeric, exhibits antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with nail fungus, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth.
remedy_constituents: Berberine: Berberine is a bioactive alkaloid that has antifungal properties, helping to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause nail fungus.
Oleanolic acid: Oleanolic acid is a triterpenoid with antifungal and antibacterial properties, contributing to its potential in treating nail fungal infections.
Vasakine: Vasakine is a phenolic compound with antifungal and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with nail fungus.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain berberine, a compound with antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Barks: They contain berberine, which is effective against fungal infections such as nail fungus.
Leaves: They are also a source of berberine, which helps to inhibit the growth of fungi causing nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of berberis aristata roots and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the roots in a hot air oven at 60°c for 6 hours or in shade for 7 days.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using a grinder at high speed for 5 minutes.
Mix 1 gram of the powder with 1 gram of excipients like starch and fill in gelatin capsules of size 0.
Seal the capsules and store them in a cool dry place for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow capsules helps with nail fungus because of its powerful antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of fungal cells that cause infection. The capsules' active compounds, such as acetic acid and chromone, work synergistically to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy environment for nail growth. Additionally, yarrow's antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects help to combat secondary bacterial infections and soothe discomfort associated with nail fungus, allowing for clear and healthy nails to grow back.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial compounds: Yarrow contains antimicrobial compounds like thymol, carvacrol, and sabinene that inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, including those causing nail fungus, helping to prevent infection and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and flavonoids, can reduce inflammation and swelling around the affected nail area, promoting a healthy environment for the nail to grow back stronger and healthier.

Wound-healing promotion: Yarrow's extracts have been shown to enhance wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, improving blood flow, and reducing scar tissue formation, which can help accelerate the growth of new, healthy nail tissue.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungal pathogens that cause nail fungus, such as Trichophyton and Candida species.

Flavonoids (including kaempferol and quercetin): As potent antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, they help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nail bed, creating an unfavorable environment for fungal growth.

Essential oil components (including camphor and bornyl acetate): These volatile compounds display antifungal activity, disrupting the cell membranes of fungal pathogens and preventing them from multiplying and infecting the nail.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are used to make capsules for nail fungus because they contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have antifungal properties.
Flowers: Flowers are used to make capsules for nail fungus because they contain flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which help inhibit fungal growth.
Stems: Stems are used to make capsules for nail fungus because they contain sesquiterpene lactones, which have antifungal properties and help in the treatment of fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried achillea millefolium flowers and leaves from a clean and well-maintained area.
Dry the harvested material in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 2-3 hours to remove moisture.
Use a coffee grinder to grind 5-10 grams of dried material into a fine powder.
Fill 10-20 empty gelatin capsules with 250-500 milligrams of the herbal powder.
Seal the capsules using a capsule sealer to maintain the herbal integrity and freshness.

The herb contains allantoin, a natural compound that stimulates cell regeneration and improves circulation, allowing for faster healing and strengthening of nails. Additionally, comfrey's antimicrobial properties help combat fungal infections, such as onychomycosis, by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic microorganisms. remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin helps to promote wound healing by stimulating collagen synthesis, which can aid in the recovery of fungal-infected nails by promoting a healthy and strong nail bed.
Saponins: Saponins have antifungal properties, which can help to combat fungal infections such as nail fungus by reducing the growth and proliferation of fungal organisms on the nail surface.

Rutin: Rutin is an antioxidant that helps to improve blood circulation and reduce inflammation in the affected area, which can promote a healthy environment for nail regrowth and prevent further infection.
remedy_constituents: Alkaloids: Alkaloids present in Symphytum officinale, such as anagyrine and symphytine, exhibit antifungal properties that can help combat nail fungus by inhibiting the growth of fungal cells.

Saponins: Saponins found in Symphytum officinale possess antifungal and antiseptic properties, which can help reduce fungal infections in the nails by creating an environment that is unfavorable for fungal growth.

Phenolic compounds: Phenolic compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, in Symphytum officinale capsules have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and antifungal properties, which can help protect the nails from fungal infections and promote overall health.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in allantoin, an active compound that helps to stimulate collagen production and promote wound healing.
Roots: They contain a high concentration of saponins, which have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat fungal infections.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried symphytum officinale root and leaves from a trusted supplier or your garden.

Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Fill 0.5-1 gram of the powdered plant material into 0.5-1 gram size vegetarian capsules using a capsule filler.
Seal the filled capsules and label them with the date and contents using a permanent marker.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain potent antifungal compounds that penetrate deeply into the nail plate, attacking the underlying fungal infection. remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Plantago major are primarily used due to their high concentration of astringent and antifungal compounds, such as aucubin and caffeic acid, which help combat fungal infections.

Crush the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powder with 100 milligrams of silica gel in a bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder-silica mixture using a capsule filler for 10 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for 6 weeks.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wintergreen capsules helps with nail fungus because of its potent antimicrobial properties. The active compound, methyl salicylate, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause nail infections. As a natural alternative to traditional medications, wintergreen capsules can help to soothe and calm the affected area, promoting healthy nail growth while also combating the underlying infection. remedy_constituents: Gaultherin: Gaultherin is a phenolic compound that exhibits antifungal properties, which can help inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens that cause nail fungus.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum capsules helps with nail fungus because of its unique combination of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. These compounds have been shown to possess antifungal properties that can help combat the infection, reduce inflammation, and promote healthy nail growth. remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a reputable supplier or grow your own plants for at least 6 months before harvest.
Grind the dried eucalyptus globulus leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle for 30 seconds to release oils and flavor compounds.
Mix 5 grams of the ground eucalyptus globulus powder with 50 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in a glass jar, stirring occasionally for 2 hours to create an infusion.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid for encapsulation, taking approximately 45 minutes.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with 200 milligrams of the eucalyptus globulus-infused olive oil, sealing the capsules immediately to preserve potency, ready for oral administration in 20 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile capsules helps with nail fungus because of its potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Wound-healing properties: Chamomile's flavonoids and terpenes promote wound healing by increasing collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and reducing inflammation, which can help to clear up fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.

remedy_recipe: Harvest dried matricaria chamomilla flowers and measure out 20 grams of the herb for use in this recipe.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle within 5 minutes.
Mix the ground matricaria chamomilla with 40 grams of tapioca starch and 10 grams of gelatin to create a uniform blend in 10 minutes.
Fill empty capsule shells with approximately 500 milligrams of the herbal blend using a spoon or measuring scoop in 2-3 minutes per shell.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to one year, away from direct sunlight and heat sources.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica archangelica roots and clean them thoroughly under running water.
Dry the cleaned roots in a warm, dry place for at least 24 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for about an hour.
Mix 250 milligrams of the powdered root with 100 milliliters of distilled water to create a decoction for 30 minutes.
Fill empty capsules with the cooled decoction and store them in an airtight container for up to three months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's wort capsules helps with nail fungus because of its antifungal properties, which can help to combat fungal infections that cause discoloration, brittleness, and crumbling of the nails. remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh st john's wort (hypericum perforatum) leaves and flowers for drying.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Grind the dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder.
Mix the powdered hypericum with 100 grams of silica gel capsules and 10 grams of organic coconut oil to form a blend.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with one level teaspoon of the blend and seal for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horse chestnut capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain a powerful ingredient called escin, which has potent antifungal properties. When taken orally, escin is absorbed into the bloodstream and travels to the affected area, where it targets the fungal infection and prevents its spread. The capsule's anti-inflammatory properties also help reduce swelling and discomfort associated with nail fungus, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth. As a result, horse chestnut capsules can provide natural relief from painful and unsightly toenail fungus.

Saponins: Saponins present in horse chestnut have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce inflammation and prevent bacterial infections that can exacerbate nail fungus.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids, a class of compounds found in horse chestnut, exhibit antioxidant properties, which can help protect the nails from oxidative stress and damage caused by fungal infections.

remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality aesculus hippocastanum bark and dry it thoroughly for at least 48 hours in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried aesculus hippocastanum bark into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or coffee grinder, taking approximately 30 minutes to achieve desired consistency.
Mix the powdered aesculus hippocastanum with gelatin capsules and distilled water in a ratio of 1:4:8 for a total of 100 capsules, allowing mixture to sit for 15 minutes before filling capsules.
Fill empty gelatin capsules using a capsule filler or spoon, aiming for a consistent weight of approximately 500 milligrams per capsule, and store filled capsules in an airtight container.
Store the finished herbal aesculus hippocastanum capsules in a cool, dark place for up to six months or freeze them for longer-term preservation, ensuring accurate dosing and potency throughout use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tomato capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain powerful antioxidants and antibacterial compounds found in tomatoes. These nutrients, such as lycopene and vitamin E, penetrate deep into the nail bed and surrounding tissues, fighting off fungal infections and promoting healthy growth. The capsules also boost the immune system, allowing the body to naturally fight off infection and reduce inflammation. As a result, herbal tomato capsules provide a natural and effective way to treat and prevent nail fungus.
remedy_properties: Tea Tree Oil: Contains antimicrobial compounds like melaleuca alternifolia, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria, helping to combat nail fungus.
Turmeric: Rich in curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and promote healing in cases of fungal infections.
Grapefruit Seed Extract: Contains compounds like limonene and citral, which have antimicrobial properties that can help combat fungal and bacterial growth, potentially treating nail fungus.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help combat fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.
Seeds: Containing high amounts of fatty acids and vitamins, they provide nourishment and moisturizing benefits to dry, brittle nails.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh solanum lycopersicum leaves and stems free from pesticides.
Dry the harvested plant material in a clean, dry place at room temperature for 7 days.
Crush the dried solanum lycopersicum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1 teaspoon of the crushed powder with 100ml of boiling water to create a decoction, steeping for 10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through cheesecloth or a coffee filter, filling capsules with 500mg of the resulting liquid extract.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that target fungal growth. The capsules also have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain associated with fungal infections. Additionally, blueberries are rich in vitamins and minerals such as vitamin C and copper, which are essential for healthy nail growth. By taking herbal blueberry capsules regularly, individuals can promote a healthy nail environment, prevent further infection, and encourage the growth of strong, fungus-free nails.
remedy_properties: Antifungal compounds: Blueberries contain various antifungal compounds, such as anthocyanins and phenolic acids, which can inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail infections.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in blueberry extract, including flavonoids and polyphenols, help to neutralize free radicals that can damage healthy nail tissue and contribute to fungal infections.

Inflammation reduction: Blueberries contain anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid and quercetin, which can reduce inflammation and irritation associated with nail fungus, promoting a healthier environment for the nails to grow.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the blueberry plant are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to combat fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.
Barks: The barks of the blueberry plant contain tannins, which have antifungal properties that can help to treat nail fungus by reducing inflammation and preventing further infection.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh vaccinium myrtillus berries or purchase dried berries from a reputable supplier.
Clean and dry the berries completely then grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 2500 milligrams of the powdered berry extract with 500 milligrams of boron powder in a small bowl.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with 750 milligrams of the berry-boron blend and seal them tightly.
Store the finished capsules in an airtight container at room temperature for up to six months.

intro_desc: Herbal capsules for nail fungus are a natural remedy that helps alleviate fungal infections of the nails by using plant extracts to combat the fungal growth. The best herbal capsules for nail fungus include Melaleuca alternifolia, Thuja occidentalis, Artemisia absinthium, Aloe barbadensis, Ocimum basilicum, Cinnamomum camphora, Curcuma longa, Eucalyptus globulus, Olea europaea, and Lavandula angustifolia. If left untreated, nail fungus can cause pain, embarrassment, and difficulties in daily activities, negatively impacting one's self-confidence and social life.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to nail fungus that are treated with herbal capsules are skin issues such as acne, eczema, and psoriasis. Herbal capsules containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and turmeric have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in these conditions. Additionally, fungal infections like athlete's foot and ringworm can also be treated with herbal remedies, including capsules containing natural antifungals like garlic and goldenseal.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal capsules that help with nail fungus is a blend of Tea Tree Oil, Turmeric, and Garlic. Tea Tree Oil's antifungal properties combat the infection, while Turmeric's curcumin reduces inflammation and promotes healing. Garlic's allicin kills fungal cells, preventing re-infection. Additionally, Olive Leaf and Oregano Oil can be added for their antifungal and antibacterial properties. This combination of herbs works synergistically to promote healthy nail growth and eliminate fungal infections, providing a natural and effective solution for nail fungus treatment. Regular use can lead to improved nail health and appearance.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain high concentrations of tea tree oil, a natural antifungal agent that targets and eliminates fungal infections. The capsules' active ingredients work to inhibit the growth and spread of fungus, promoting healthy nail growth and restoring clear, fungus-free nails. Studies have shown that the antifungal properties of tea tree oil can effectively treat nail fungus, reducing symptoms such as thickening, discoloration, and brittleness. Regular use can lead to significant improvements in nail health and appearance.
remedy_constituents: Catechins: These plant compounds help with nail fungus by exhibiting antifungal and antimicrobial properties, inhibiting the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause infections.
Cineole (Eucalyptol): This compound contributes to the antifungal effects of Melaleuca alternifolia by disrupting the cell membranes of fungi, ultimately preventing their growth and spread.
Cymene: This terpene has shown antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help in treating nail fungus by suppressing the growth of fungi and preventing the formation of fungal spores.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make nail fungus capsules due to their high concentration of tea tree oil, which has antifungal properties.
Buds: The buds of Melaleuca alternifolia are used to make nail fungus capsules as they contain a high amount of tea tree oil, which helps in treating fungal infections.
Leaves: The leaves are also used for making nail fungus capsules as they contain compounds that inhibit the growth of fungi.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried leaves from melaleuca alternifolia plants on a sunny day.
Dry the harvested leaves further in a low-temperature oven at 50 degrees celsius for 2 hours.
Grind 10 grams of the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix 1 gram of the powdered melaleuca alternifolia with 3 grams of gelatin and 2 grams of water to create a uniform blend.
Encapsulate the blend using a capsule machine with a 00 size capsule for 10 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thuja occidentalis capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain the herb's active compounds that have antifungal properties. These compounds, such as thujone and other terpenoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause nail fungus. By targeting the fungal infection at its source, Thuja occidentalis capsules may help to reduce inflammation, promote a healthy nail environment, and ultimately lead to the resolution of fungal infections. This natural approach can be a promising alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Thujone has antifungal properties, which help inhibit the growth and spread of fungi that cause nail fungus.
Cadinene: Cadinene is a sesquiterpene that has been shown to have antifungal and antiseptic properties, which help to combat fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.
Cadinol: Cadinol is another sesquiterpene that has been found to have antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent the growth of fungi and bacteria that can cause nail fungus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in essential oils, particularly thujone, which has antifungal properties.
Seeds: They contain thujone and other compounds that may help combat fungal infections.
Barks: The bark of Thuja occidentalis contains compounds with antifungal properties, making it useful for treating nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh thuja occidentalis leaves in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a high-speed grinder.
Mix 500 milligrams of the powder with 500 milligrams of vegetable cellulose to create capsules.
Fill gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain wormwood oil, which has antifungal properties that can inhibit the growth of fungal spores. The oil's compounds, such as thujone and borneol, have been shown to exhibit antiseptic and antimicrobial activity, reducing the severity of fungal infections. By using Artemisia absinthium capsules, individuals can promote a healthy nail environment, prevent further fungal growth, and potentially reverse the symptoms of nail fungus, allowing for stronger and healthier nails to grow.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: It has antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of fungi causing nail fungus.
Absinthin: It has antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the infection and promote healthy nail growth.
Ligustilide: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and discomfort caused by nail fungus, promoting a healthy environment for nail growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Artemisia absinthium leaves are used to make capsules for nail fungus treatment due to their high content of sesquiterpenes, which have antifungal properties.
Stems: The stems of Artemisia absinthium are used in capsules to treat nail fungus due to their ability to inhibit fungal growth and reduce inflammation.
Buds: Artemisia absinthium buds are utilized in capsules for nail fungus treatment because they contain compounds that have been shown to have antifungal and antibacterial properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry the herbs by spreading them in a single layer on paper trays in a low-temperature oven at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried herbs into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Mix the ground herbs with a small amount of gelatin or plant-derived capsule filler in a ratio of 1 part herb to 1 part filler.
Fill empty vegetable capsules with the herb-filler mixture using a powder filling machine or a spoon for 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place for up to 1 year.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain the powerful anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties of Aloe vera. The gel within the plant has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause nail fungus, while also soothing and calming the skin around the affected area. By promoting healthy nail growth and reducing inflammation, Aloe barbadensis capsules can help to clear up fungal infections and promote a healthy, fungus-free nail. Regular use can lead to improved nail health and appearance.
remedy_constituents: Polysaccharides: These complex carbohydrates have anti-fungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungi that cause nail infections.
Acemannan: This type of polysaccharide has anti-inflammatory and anti-microbial properties, which help to reduce fungal growth and promote a healthy nail environment.
Alizarin: This anthraquinone compound has anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to inhibit fungal growth and reduce inflammation associated with nail fungus.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of aloin and aloe-emodin, which have antifungal properties that help combat nail fungus.
Seeds: They are used for their oil content, which has anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties that aid in treating nail fungus.
Barks: They are used due to their high concentration of anthraquinones, which have antiseptic and antifungal properties that help prevent the spread of nail fungus.
remedy_recipe: Harvest the aloe leaves from mature plants with thick fleshy leaves that are free of any damage or disease.
Extract the gel from the aloe leaves using a juicer or blender to obtain 1000 milliliters of gel.
Filter the gel through a cheesecloth or a fine mesh to remove any impurities or sediment.
Use a vacuum dryer to dry the gel at a temperature of 50 to 60 degrees celsius for 24 to 48 hours.
Fill gelatin capsules with 0.5 to 1 gram of dried aloe gel powder and seal them tightly.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ocimum basilicum capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain the antibacterial and antifungal properties of basil, which effectively combat fungal infections. The active compounds in basil, such as eugenol and linalool, inhibit the growth of fungi, promoting a healthy nail environment. Additionally, basil's antioxidant properties help to boost the immune system, enabling it to fight off fungal infections more efficiently. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative to traditional treatments for nail fungus.
remedy_constituents: Borneol: This constituent has antifungal properties that help inhibit the growth of fungi causing nail infections.
Linalool: It has a fungicidal effect, which can help kill fungal cells causing nail fungus, and its antiseptic properties can reduce inflammation.
Eugenol: This constituent has antifungal, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties that can help combat fungal infections, reduce inflammation, and promote healing in the affected nail area.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves are the most commonly used part of Ocimum basilicum for nail fungus treatment due to their high concentration of essential oils and antioxidants.
Seeds: The seeds are also used, as they contain a higher concentration of volatile oils, which have been shown to have antifungal properties.
Stems: The stems, although less commonly used than leaves and seeds, are still used in some formulations due to their potential antifungal and antimicrobial properties.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of fresh ocimum basilicum leaves in a low-temperature oven at 40°c for 24 hours.
Grind 200g of dried ocimum basilicum leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 2g of powder with 10g of gelatin and 10g of glycerin to form a paste.
Fill 100 capsules with 0.25g of the paste and seal them using an encapsulation machine.
Label 50 capsules with the name ocimum basilicum and store them in an airtight container for 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinnamomum camphora capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain camphor, which is a potent antifungal agent that effectively targets and kills fungal spores. The capsules also contain eucalyptol, a compound that helps to reduce inflammation and promote healthy nail growth. By penetrating deep into the nail bed, camphor and eucalyptol work synergistically to eliminate fungal infections, promoting a healthy and fungus-free nail environment. This makes Cinnamomum camphora capsules a popular natural remedy for nail fungus treatment.
remedy_constituents: Cinnamaldehyde: It helps with nail fungus by exhibiting antifungal properties and inhibiting the growth of fungal pathogens, such as Trichophyton and Candida.
Limonene: It helps with nail fungus by demonstrating antifungal and antibacterial activities, which can help prevent the spread of fungal infections and promote a healthy nail environment.
α-Terpineol: It helps with nail fungus by exhibiting antifungal properties, reducing inflammation, and promoting the healing of infected nails.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Cinnamomum camphora are used due to their high content of cinnamaldehyde, which has antifungal properties that can help combat nail fungus.
Barks: The barks of Cinnamomum camphora are used because they contain compounds that have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi and promote healthy nail growth.
Roots: The roots of Cinnamomum camphora are used for their antifungal and antibacterial properties, which help to prevent the spread of nail fungus and promote a healthy nail environment.
remedy_recipe: Dry 500g of cinnamomum camphora bark in a dehydrator at 40°c for 48 hours.
Grind the dried cinnamomum camphora bark into a fine powder using a coffee grinder.
Mix 250g of the powdered cinnamomum camphora bark with 250g of tapioca starch.
Fill 500 empty gelatin capsules with 0.5g of the cinnamomum camphora-tapioca starch mixture each.
Store the cinnamomum camphora capsules in an airtight container in a cool dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa capsules helps with nail fungus because of its potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. Curcumin, the active compound found in Turmeric, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi that cause nail infections. It also helps to reduce inflammation and promote healthy nail growth. Studies have demonstrated that Curcuma longa can effectively reduce the severity of nail fungus symptoms, such as discoloration and brittleness, and promote clear and healthy nails. This natural remedy has been used for centuries in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various skin and nail conditions.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It helps with nail fungus by exhibiting potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and reduce inflammation in the affected nail area.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It contributes to the antifungal activity of Curcuma longa by inhibiting the synthesis of ergosterol, an essential component of fungal cell membranes, thereby disrupting fungal cell growth and viability.

Tumerone (specifically alpha- and beta-tumerones): These sesquiterpenes in Curcuma longa help with nail fungus by exhibiting antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which prevent the overgrowth of fungal pathogens and promote a healthy nail environment.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound with potent antifungal properties.
Roots: They are used due to their ability to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens, making them an effective treatment for nail fungus.
Stems: They are utilized due to their content of bioactive compounds that help to prevent fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.
remedy_recipe: Collect 1 kilogram of dried curcuma longa rhizomes from a reputable source for medicinal purposes.
Grind the dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 10 minutes.
Mix 250 grams of the ground powder with 250 grams of tapioca starch in a bowl for 5 minutes.
Use a capsule machine to fill empty capsules with 0.5 grams of the powder mixture per capsule in 30 minutes.
Store the filled capsules in a cool dark place to preserve their potency for up to 2 years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain the essential oils extracted from the leaves of the Eucalyptus plant. These oils, particularly cineole, have potent antifungal properties that inhibit the growth of fungal cells. By penetrating deep into the nail bed, they help to reduce the severity of fungal infections, such as onychomycosis, and promote a healthy nail growth environment. Regular use of Eucalyptus globulus capsules may lead to improved nail health and a reduction in fungal nail infections.
remedy_constituents: Cineole: This constituent helps with nail fungus by exhibiting antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungal pathogens.
Caffeic acid: This constituent helps with nail fungus by reducing fungal colonization and preventing the formation of fungal biofilms.
Eudesmol: This constituent helps with nail fungus by exhibiting antifungal and antibacterial properties, creating an environment that is less conducive to fungal growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high oil content, particularly eucalyptol, which has antifungal and antibacterial properties.
Barks: They are used for their ability to inhibit the growth of fungi and promote healthy nail growth, thanks to their tannin and flavonoid content.
Stems: They are used due to their richness in eucalyptol and other essential oils, which can help to eliminate fungal infections and promote nail health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves from a reputable source within 6 months.
Dry the leaves further in a single layer at 105 degrees fahrenheit for 2 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 30 seconds.
Mix the powder with 500 milligrams of vegetable capsules and 100 milligrams of silica filler.
Fill the capsules manually or using a capsule filler machine within 1 hour.

remedies.remedy_desc: Olea europaea capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain the extract of olive leaves, rich in oleuropein, a powerful antifungal agent. This natural compound effectively inhibits the growth of fungus, such as Trichophyton and Candida, that causes nail infections. By promoting a healthy nail environment, Olea europaea capsules support the body's natural defenses and encourage the growth of strong, fungus-free nails. As a result, nail fungus symptoms like discoloration and brittleness can be alleviated, leading to healthier and more attractive nails.
remedy_constituents: Oleanolic acid: It has antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of fungi causing nail fungus.
Tyrosol: It exhibits antifungal and antioxidant activities, contributing to the prevention of fungal infections and promotion of healthy nail growth.
Hydroxytyrosol: It possesses potent antifungal and antimicrobial properties, helping to combat fungal infections and promote a healthy nail environment.
remedy_parts: Olive oil (Seeds): The seeds are used to produce olive oil, which is often extracted and used as a main ingredient in nail fungus capsules due to its antifungal properties.

Oleuropein (Leaves): The leaves are a rich source of oleuropein, a compound that has been shown to have antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it a popular ingredient in nail fungus treatments.

Oleanolic acid (Barks): The bark of the olive tree is a rich source of oleanolic acid, a compound that has been shown to have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a useful ingredient in nail fungus treatments.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of dried olea europaea leaves and 100 grams of vegetable cellulose powder.
Mix the dried olea europaea leaves with 50 grams of starch and 20 grams of silica gel.
Add the vegetable cellulose powder to the mixture and stir until well combined.
Fill 100-count capsules with the herbal mixture and seal with a capsule machine.
Label and date the capsules and store them in a cool dry place for up to two years.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia capsules helps with nail fungus because they contain the essential oils of the lavender plant, which have antifungal properties. The capsules can help to inhibit the growth of fungal organisms that cause nail fungus, such as Trichophyton and Candida. The antiseptic and antimicrobial properties of lavender oil can also help to reduce inflammation and promote a healthy environment for nail growth, making it easier to treat and prevent nail fungus. This natural remedy can be a useful alternative to conventional treatments.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has antifungal properties, inhibiting the growth of fungi that cause nail fungus.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene also has antifungal properties, helping to reduce fungal infections and promote healing.
Caffeic acid: This phenolic compound has antimicrobial properties, which can help combat fungal infections and promote healthy nail growth.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make capsules for nail fungus due to their high content of essential oils with antifungal properties.
Flowers: They are used to make capsules for nail fungus due to their ability to inhibit the growth of fungal cells.
Seeds: They are used to make capsules for nail fungus due to their rich antioxidant and antifungal properties that aid in treating fungal infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers in the morning after the dew has dried completely taking about 30 minutes.
Dry the harvested flowers in a warm dry place for 1 to 2 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Grind the dried flowers into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle for 2 minutes.
Mix the powder with a suitable capsule filler material in a 1:1 ratio by weight for 5 minutes.
Fill empty gelatin capsules with the powder mixture and seal them immediately to preserve potency within 10 minutes.
